# MarketerOps.ai — Project Context for LLMs

## What This Project Is

MarketerOps.ai is a Korean-market SaaS for marketers.
It provides AI-powered SEO, GEO (Generative Engine Optimization), content generation, and keyword analysis in a single dashboard.
The target user is a 1-person in-house marketer or startup growth lead who needs senior-level marketing insights without an agency.

---

## Tech Stack

- Framework: Next.js 16 with App Router (NOT Pages Router)
- Language: TypeScript 5
- Styling: Tailwind CSS v4 (utility-first, no CSS modules, no styled-components)
- Icons: lucide-react
- Charts: recharts (AreaChart, RadarChart)
- AI: Google Gemini 2.5 Flash via @google/generative-ai
- HTML parsing: cheerio (server-side only)
- Data persistence: localStorage via lib/storage.ts (no database yet)
- Deployment: Vercel (Fluid Compute, Node.js runtime)
- Node version: 24 LTS

---

## Directory Structure

```
/
├── app/
│   ├── layout.tsx              # Root layout (html, body, globals.css)
│   ├── page.tsx                # Single-page app shell — sidebar, tabs, toast
│   ├── globals.css             # Global styles + Tailwind directives
│   └── api/
│       ├── analyze/route.ts    # POST — PageSpeed Insights API v5 (mobile + desktop)
│       ├── geo/route.ts        # POST — GEO visibility analysis via HTML parsing
│       ├── advice/route.ts     # POST — Gemini AI senior marketer diagnosis report
│       ├── content/route.ts    # POST — Gemini AI content generation (blog, SNS, CTA)
│       └── keyword/route.ts    # POST — Gemini AI keyword analysis
├── components/
│   ├── DiagnosisModule.tsx     # Tab 1: SEO + GEO diagnosis UI
│   ├── ContentHubModule.tsx    # Tab 2: Content generation UI
│   ├── KeywordModule.tsx       # Tab 3: Keyword analysis UI
│   └── DashboardModule.tsx     # Tab 4: Analytics dashboard (charts, history)
├── lib/
│   └── storage.ts              # localStorage helpers: saveDiagnosis, getDiagnoses, getStats, incrementContentCount
├── public/                     # Static assets
├── CLAUDE.md                   # Points to AGENTS.md
├── AGENTS.md                   # LLM agent instructions
├── master_plan.md              # Product vision, feature roadmap, tech decisions
├── todo_list.md                # Done / in-progress / backlog tasks
└── llms.txt                    # This file — project context for AI assistants
```

---

## Conventions

### Code Style

- No comments unless the WHY is non-obvious
- No docstrings or multi-line comment blocks
- Prefer string concatenation over nested template literals (MCP file push corrupts nested backticks)
- TypeScript interfaces defined at the top of the file that uses them
- No barrel exports (import directly from the file)
- `'use client'` at the top of every component file

### API Routes

- All routes are POST with JSON body
- Validate input at the top, return 400 for missing params
- Use string concatenation (NOT template literals) for Gemini prompts
- Always use model `gemini-2.5-flash`
- Strip markdown code fences from Gemini responses before JSON.parse:
  ```ts
  text.replace(/^```json\s*/i, '').replace(/^```\s*/i, '').replace(/\s*```$/i, '').trim()
  ```
- Return errors as `{ error: 'message' }` with appropriate HTTP status

### Components

- Shared UI primitives (Card, Badge, ScoreRing) defined inline at the top of the file that uses them — do not extract to separate files unless used in 3+ files
- Color coding for scores: `>= 80` → emerald, `>= 50` → amber, `< 50` → rose
- onToast prop pattern: `({ onToast }: { onToast: (msg: string) => void })`
- Loading state uses `Loader2` with `animate-spin` from lucide-react
- Error state uses a rose-colored div with `AlertCircle` icon

### Styling

- Max width containers: `max-w-5xl mx-auto` (most modules), `max-w-6xl mx-auto` (dashboard)
- Card base: `bg-white border border-slate-200 rounded-2xl shadow-sm overflow-hidden`
- Primary action button: `bg-indigo-600 hover:bg-indigo-700 disabled:bg-slate-300 text-white font-bold rounded-2xl`
- Input field: `border border-slate-200 rounded-2xl focus:ring-2 focus:ring-indigo-500 focus:border-transparent outline-none`
- Animation on result reveal: `animate-in fade-in slide-in-from-bottom-4 duration-500`

### Data / Storage

- `lib/storage.ts` is the single source of truth for localStorage keys
- DIAGNOSIS_KEY = `'marketerops_diagnoses'` (max 20 records, newest first)
- STATS_KEY = `'marketerops_stats'` (diagnosisCount, contentCount)
- Always guard with `if (typeof window === 'undefined') return` for SSR safety

### Git & Deployment

- Production branch: `main` (triggers Vercel auto-deploy)
- Feature branches: `claude/*`
- NEVER use nested template literals in files pushed via MCP
- ALWAYS run `npm run build` locally before pushing to main
- Use `mcp__github__create_or_update_file` (not `push_files`) for safe GitHub updates
- SHA required for existing files: `git rev-parse origin/main:<path>`

---

## Environment Variables

| Variable | Required | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| GEMINI_API_KEY | Yes | Google Gemini AI (all AI features) |
| PAGESPEED_API_KEY | No | PageSpeed Insights (works without key, lower rate limit) |

---

## Key Business Logic

### GEO Score Calculation (app/api/geo/route.ts)

Starts at 100, deducts points for each issue:
- No llms.txt: -20
- No JSON-LD: -20 / wrong types: -10
- AI bots blocked in robots.txt: -15
- No meta description: -10
- No canonical URL: -5
- Missing image alt attributes: -2 per image, max -10
- No H1 tag: -10

### Diagnosis Storage (lib/storage.ts)

Saves mobile Performance, SEO, Accessibility scores from PageSpeed + GEO score from HTML analysis.
Dashboard reads this data to render charts and history.

### Content Generation Flow (ContentHubModule → /api/content)

Input: keyword, target audience, tone (professional/friendly/casual/authoritative)
Output: 3 blog titles, 5-step blog outline, 3 SNS captions, 3 CTAs

### Keyword Analysis Flow (KeywordModule → /api/keyword)

Input: single keyword string
Output: intent (정보형/거래형/탐색형/비교형), difficulty (Low/Medium/High), monthly volume estimate, 8 related keywords, 4 long-tail keywords, 3 content angles, 3 SEO titles, 1 meta description

---

## What NOT To Do

- Do not use Pages Router (`pages/` directory)
- Do not use Edge Runtime (cheerio requires Node.js)
- Do not add a database without updating storage.ts interface first
- Do not create new shared component files unless 3+ modules need it
- Do not use nested template literals in API route prompts
- Do not push to main without a local build check
